articles.title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
articles.description: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade. The details were thin, and many people were unconvinced. But Subquadratic has sta

articles.title: Evaporative Cooling in Data Centers: Why the Industry Hesitates to Move On
articles.description: Evaporative cooling still dominates data centers, but with rising liquid cooling adoption, is the industry ready to embrace more sustainable solutions?

articles.title: The inevitable weakness of metrics
articles.description: There are plenty of useful things a metric can reveal. There are even more it can obscure or corrupt. articles.title: Brain-computer interface trials are taking off
articles.description: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. articles.title: FERC Targets Grid Rules for Data Centers and Large Loads
articles.description: FERC gave six grid operators 60 days to justify or revise large-load tariffs and 30 days to explain how they will power growing AI demand.

articles.title: Battery Storage Moves Closer to Data Centers, but Challenges Persist
articles.description: Operators are deploying battery systems to support reliability and grid flexibility, but cost, performance, and complexity still hinder adoption.

articles.description: Amazon and Google’s multibillion-dollar data center investments in Montgomery County are positioning Missouri as a key destination for hyperscalers seeking power and scalability.
